english: to pat; to beat; to take (a picture)

etymology.description: semantic 扌(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 白(白) (bái, "white") -> hand action -> to pat; to beat
etymology.interpretation: 拍(pāi, "to pat; to beat; to take (a picture)") combines the semantic component 扌(手) (shǒu, "hand"), indicating movement or using the hand, with the phonetic component 白(白) (bái). The action of a hand repeatedly contacting a surface naturally conveys the sense of 'pat' or 'beat'. This character was adapted for the meaning 'to take (a picture)' as taking a photograph is a rapid capturing process similar to a pat.

examples.chinese: 他轻轻地拍他的肩膀(他輕輕地拍他的肩膀)。
examples.pinyin: Tā qīngqīng de pāi tā de jiānbǎng.

examples.chinese: 我今天拍了一张很漂亮的照片(我今天拍了一張很漂亮的照片)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ jīntiān pāi le yī zhāng hěn piàoliang de zhàopiàn.
examples.english: I took a very beautiful picture today.

examples.chinese: 摄影师在公园拍风景照片(攝影師在公園拍風景照片)。
examples.pinyin: Shèyǐngshī zài gōngyuán pāi fēngjǐng zhàopiàn.
examples.english: The photographer takes landscape photos in the park.
